Background
About Shivam Developers

Shivam Developers, a Pune-based construction firm founded in 2012, specializes in industrial and residential projects across Maharashtra. Initially focused on land development—contouring, excavation, leveling, pitching—they expanded into turnkey construction services in 2016, delivering over ten medium-to-large-scale industrial sheds and office buildings ranging from 19,000 sq ft to 68,000 sq ft sites. To streamline their operations, reduce material waste, and accelerate project timelines, Shivam Developers adopted SiteFlow’s construction management platform, concentrating on its Material Management and Task Progress Tracking modules.

Challenge
What was holding them back
cascade classes removed; ::before ⚠ → inline span
Problem 01  ·  Inefficient Material Tracking
Limited visibility into on-site material consumption

Materials were procured in bulk—ready-mix concrete grades M10–M40, structural steel—with limited visibility into on-site consumption. Inventory discrepancies led to over-ordering (up to 12% excess) and occasional job delays waiting for replenishment.

⚠Up to 12% material over-ordering
Problem 02  ·  Manual Progress Reporting
Paper logs and spreadsheets causing 6–8 hrs of admin per week

Supervisors logged daily task updates on paper, then collated them into spreadsheets. This process consumed 6–8 hours per week and introduced data-entry errors, making it hard to pinpoint schedule slippages in real-time.

⚠8 hrs/week lost to manual reporting
Script removed — only purpose was scroll cascade Part A: Solution
cascade classes removed from all elements
Solution
SiteFlow implemented two core modules for Shivam Developers
ModuleKey Capabilities
Material ManagementReal-time inventory dashboards; automated reordering alerts, batch-level tracking
Task Progress TrackingMobile field updates; Gantt-style dashboards, alerting on slippages
📊 Material Management Solution
✓
Batch-level Visibility: Each concrete pour (e.g., M25 batches for a 50,000 sq ft industrial shed) was scanned via QR codes. SiteFlow logged volumes used versus planned, flagging variances above 5%.
✓
Automated Alerts: When stock dipped below reorder thresholds, SiteFlow alerted procurement, eliminating emergency orders.
✓
Waste Reduction: By reconciling planned versus actual usage daily, Shivam Developers cut material over-consumption by 14%.
✅ Progress & Task Management Solution
✓
Mobile Check-ins: Field engineers updated task status from smartphones. Progress on formwork, reinforcement, and finishing phases synced instantly.
✓
Proactive Notifications: When a critical-path activity lagged by more than 2 days, SiteFlow sent SMS/email alerts to stakeholders.
✓
Real-Time Dashboards: Project managers viewed live Gantt charts, highlighting tasks behind schedule.
Part B: Results
Results & ROI
Measurable impact across every project
MetricBeforeAfterImprovement
Material cost over-run12% of budget2% of budget↓ 83%
Weekly admin time for progress reports8 hours2 hours↓ 75%
Schedule delays (average per project)7 days1.5 days↓ 79%
6 hrsTime Savings

Supervisory staff reclaimed 6 hours weekly, redirecting effort toward quality checks and client coordination.

±2 daysOn-Time Delivery

Projects now finish within 2 days of planned completion—boosting client satisfaction and repeat business.

₹1.2MCost Savings

By cutting material over-runs from 12% to 2%, Shivam Developers saved approximately ₹1.2 million on a typical ₹10 million project.
